questions.question: What is the primary difference between Traditional Business Intelligence and Big Data?
options: Traditional BI focuses on historical data analysis, while Big Data is focused on real-time insights
Traditional BI uses structured data, whereas Big Data uses unstructured data
Big Data is a subset of Business Intelligence
Traditional BI is a newer technology than Big Data
questions.explanation: Traditional Business Intelligence focuses on historical data analysis, whereas Big Data is focused on real-time insights. It uses structured data, whereas Big Data uses unstructured data.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the primary goal of Traditional Business Intelligence?
options: To provide real-time insights
To analyze large amounts of unstructured data
To support decision-making with historical data
To predict future trends
questions.explanation: Traditional Business Intelligence aims to support decision-making with historical data. It aims to provide insights that are based on past data.

questions.question: What is a key characteristic of Big Data?
options: It is easily stored and retrieved
It is highly structured and organized
It is a large and complex dataset
It is a subset of Business Intelligence
questions.explanation: Big Data is characterized by being a large and complex dataset. It is often difficult to store, process, and retrieve.

questions.question: Why is Big Data considered a game-changer in the business world?
options: It provides historical data insights
It allows for real-time decision-making
It enables organizations to make better decisions with complex datasets
It is a newer technology than Traditional Business Intelligence
questions.explanation: Big Data enables organizations to make better decisions with complex datasets. It provides a more complete understanding of the business and allows for more accurate predictions.

questions.question: What is a major challenge faced by organizations when adopting Big Data?
options: Lack of skilled professionals
Difficulty in storing and retrieving large datasets
High upfront costs
Limited data quality
questions.explanation: One of the major challenges faced by organizations when adopting Big Data is a lack of skilled professionals. This includes data scientists, analysts, and engineers who can handle large datasets.
